premise: I cleaned offices at night for 15 years to repay $85,000 in parent PLUS loans for my son’s medical degree, trusting his promise I’d live with him once he finished his residency. The week he became a cardiologist, he and his wife forged my signature on a loan-consolidation document, filed a restraining order claiming mental instability, and sold the house I’d helped fund — telling the family I’d embezzled his tuition. At 55, I became a paralegal, proved the forgery, sued for fraud, and now garnish 40% of his attending-physician salary every month; I’ve already recouped $210,000 and mail him a holiday card from my paid-off cottage each year.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Son Forged My Signature, Sold My Home, And Lied To Everyone. So I Now Get 40%
